D11 ComputationalL3 ApplyReference

Gaussian Blur — σ Controls Spatial Smoothing

2D Gaussian kernel exp(−(x²+y²)/2σ²) convolved with the image; σ sets blur radius — separable, isotropic, the workhorse low-pass for vision pipelines

Gaussian is the unique filter that simultaneously minimises space-frequency spread and is separable (1D × 1D), making it O(σ) rather than O(σ²). σ ≈ 1 px removes single-pixel noise; σ ≈ 3 px blurs textures; σ > 6 destroys fine detail. Convolution preserves total energy.

Slide σ to compare blur intensity on a synthetic test scene.
